mnemonics: RTL Is Like A Recipe: It lists the ingredients (registers), the steps (transfers), and the timing (clock cycles) to make the digital dish (result).
keyPoints: A register transfer is a controlled movement of data between registers, often synchronized by a clock pulse.
Transfer operations are represented by a replacement operator (e.g., R2 ← R1) and are executed in parallel.
Control functions, often conditional (e.g., if K1: R2 ← R1), determine when a transfer occurs.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the transfer is not instantaneous; it happens on the active clock edge after the control condition is true.
Assuming the source register is overwritten or cleared during a transfer; it is only read from, its contents remain unchanged.
Confusing the direction of the arrow; data flows from the source on the right to the destination on the left of the ← operator.
